title: Cystopteris tenuis
intro: Cystopteris tenuis, also known as slender bladder fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Cystopteris tenuis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Cystopteris tenuis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Cystopteris tenuis is a species of fern in the family Cystopteridaceae. It is a type of lacy fern native to North America, Europe, and Asia. The species is often found growing in moist, shaded areas, such as woodlands and along streams. Cystopteris tenuis is classified as a member of the genus Cystopteris, which includes several species of lacy ferns. This classification is based on the fern's unique characteristics, including its delicate fronds and chromosome number.
